codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested sync command shape, tests, and output modes, but two correctness gaps remain: the `--repo` flag is wired as both the remote destination and the local source path, and host filtering can return a false success when every requested host name is unknown.

issues.description: `syncHelp()` documents `--repo` as the path to the skills repo on remote hosts (`apps/cli/src/cli.ts:194`), but `runSyncCommand` also derives `localRepoPath` from that same flag and passes it as both the local and remote repo arguments to `runSync` (`apps/cli/src/cli.ts:551-559`). As a result, `fleet sync <skill> --repo <remote-path>` validates `<remote-path>/<skill>` on the local machine and fails before SSH whenever the remote destination differs from the local repo layout. The command therefore only works when local and remote repos happen to share the same path, which is narrower than the advertised behavior.

When host filters are supplied, `runSync` silently drops unknown host names with `allHosts.filter(([name]) => filterHosts.includes(name))` and then computes `allSucceeded` with `results.every(...)` (`apps/cli/src/commands/sync.ts:129-144`). If all requested host names are invalid, `targetHosts` is empty, `allSucceeded` becomes `true`, and the CLI exits 0 after reporting a 0-host success. That produces a false positive for typos or stale host names instead of surfacing that nothing was synced.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `cli-worker` skill's implementation guidance is stale for this repo: it still tells workers to add the command parser using commander.js or yargs, even though the CLI intentionally uses a hand-rolled parser. Workers are correctly editing the existing parser, so `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` can read as true even when the written procedure does not match reality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/cli-worker/SKILL.md:62` says to add the parser using commander.js or yargs, while `apps/cli/src/cli.ts:4-5` explicitly documents the established hand-rolled parser approach. The reviewed handoff still records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` (`/home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-15T22-06-34-794Z__cli-sync-command__2bf39f85-d700-433d-87b7-de761efd8eb2.json:167`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared manual-verification guidance for write-path CLI commands is inconsistent: the cli-worker skill says to test the actual CLI against real hosts, but the mission/library docs say the existing skills repos are read-only and that write-path checks must use isolated sandboxes. That mismatch led this worker to record a real `fleet sync weather shuvtest` run against the default repo while still marking the skill procedure as followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/cli-worker/SKILL.md:81-92` instructs real-host manual CLI checks; `/home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/AGENTS.md:16` says existing skills repos on test hosts are read-only and isolated directories must be used; `.factory/library/user-testing.md:53-74` likewise requires isolated test directories and says never to modify `~/repos/shuvbot-skills`; the reviewed handoff logs `node ./apps/cli/bin/fleet.js sync weather shuvtest` and `... --json` (`.../handoffs/2026-03-15T22-06-34-794Z__cli-sync-command__2bf39f85-d700-433d-87b7-de761efd8eb2.json:61-66`).

summary: Reviewed commit `881ab08` and the worker transcript for `cli-sync-command`. The review fails because the command misuses `--repo` as both remote destination and local source path, and because an all-unknown host filter incorrectly reports success instead of an error. I also noted two shared-state gaps in the CLI worker guidance: stale parser instructions and conflicting write-path manual-verification guidance.
